Reconcile the following columns. Document has 7 pages. Sources are anonymous (A and B).

---
Column 1: Region - N (%) | North America | Treatment
Definition: What number and percentage of participants from North America included in the treatment arm of the clinical trial? If reported, include count and/or percentage. If multiple relevant arms are reported, list each arm separately. Use 'Not reported' if missing.
  A: value="0 (0%)" | page=1 | modality=text | reasoning="The study is explicitly stated as a 'French multicenter open-label randomized study' and all listed affiliations for the authors are in France, indicating all participants were from Europe. Therefore, 0 participants were from North America."
  B: value="Not reported" | page=None | modality=text | reasoning="No information found regarding participants from North America in the treatment arm."

Platinum Priority – Prostate Cancer
Editorial by Srikala S. Sridhar and Christopher J. Sweeney on pp. 263–264 of this issue



Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus
ADT Alone in Metastatic Non castrate Prostate Cancer:
Impact of Metastatic Burden and Long-term Survival
Analysis of the Randomized Phase 3 GETUG-AFU15 Trial



Gwenaelle Gravis a,*, Jean-Marie Boher b, Florence Joly c, Michel Soulié d, Laurence Albiges e,
Franck Priou f, Igor Latorzeff g, Remy Delva h, Ivan Krakowski i, Brigitte Laguerre j,
Frédéric Rolland k, Christine Théodore l, Gael Deplanque m, Jean-Marc Ferrero n,
Stéphane Culine o, Loïc Mourey p, Philippe Beuzeboc q, Muriel Habibian r,
Stéphane Oudard s, Karim Fizazi e,



on behalf of the GETUG

ª Medical Oncology, Institut Paoli-Calmettes, Marseille, France; ᵇ Biostatistics, Institut Paoli-Calmettes, Marseille, France; Aix-Marseille Université, UMR_S 912 (SESSTIM), IRD, Marseille, France; INSERM, UMR_S 912 (SESSTIM), Marseille, France; ᶜ Medical Oncology, Centre François Baclesse - CHU Côte de Nacre, Caen, France; ᵈ Urology Department, Centre Hospitalier Universitaire Rangueil, Toulouse, France; ᵉ Department of Cancer Medicine, Institut Gustave Roussy, University of Paris Sud, Villejuif, France; ᶠ Medical Oncology, Centre Hospitalier Les Oudairies, La Roche-sur-Yon, France; ᵍ Radiotherapy Department, Clinique Pasteur, Toulouse, France; ʰ Department of Medical Oncology, Centre Paul Papin, Angers, France; ⁱ Medical Oncology, Centre Alexis Vautrin, Vandoeuvre-les-Nancy, France; ʲ Medical Oncology, Centre Eugène Marquis, Rennes, France; ᵏ Medical Oncology, Centre René Gauducheau, Saint-Herblain, France; ˡ Medical Oncology, Hôpital Foch, Suresnes, France; ᵐ Medical Oncology, Groupe Hospitalier Saint Joseph, Paris, France; ⁿ Medical Oncology, Centre Antoine Lacassagne, Nice, France; ᵒ Medical Oncology, Hôpital Saint-Louis, Paris, France; ᵖ Institut Claudius Régaud, Toulouse, France; ᑫ Medical Oncology, Institut Curie, Paris, France; ʳ UNICANCER, Paris, France; ˢ Medical Oncology Department, Georges Pompidou Hospital and René Descartes University, Paris, France



**Article info**
---
*Article history:*
Accepted November 2, 2015

*Associate Editor:*
James Catto



Keywords:
Androgen deprivation therapy
Docetaxel
Metastatic noncastrate prostate
cancer
Metastatic volume



# Abstract

**Background:** The role of chemotherapy in metastatic non castrate prostate cancer (mNCPC) is debated. Survival benefits of docetaxel (D) added to androgen-deprivation therapy (ADT) were shown in the CHAARTED trial in patients with metastatic high-volume disease (HVD).
**Objective:** To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.
**Design, setting, and participants:** Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr.
**Outcome measurements and statistical analysis:** Primary end point was OS. Secondary end points were biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS). Retrospective analysis was by tumor volume.
**Results and limitations:** After a median follow-up of 83.9 mo, median OS in the overall population was 62.1 mo (95% confidence interval [CI], 49.5–73.7) and 48.6 mo (95% CI, 40.9–60.6) for ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively (hazard ratio [HR]: 0.88 [95% CI, 0.68–1.14]; p = 0.3). Median OS in ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively, was for HVD

Tel. +33 4 91 22 37 40; Fax: +33 4 91 22 36 18. E-mail address: gravisg@ipc.unicancer.fr (G. Gravis).
